paperclip recommendation: Yes — the existing advisor flow already has distinct phases, so the shared model should be **phase-aware**, not one flat universal label set. ## Current advisor phases in the repo From `packages/advisor/src/extension.ts`: - `before_agent_start` → **preflight** - `turn_end` → **post-turn review** - `agent_end` → **final review / closeout** That means the shared pretrained model should take the current phase as input and emit different decisions depending on where it is being used. ## Recommended label structure ### 1) Preflight / escalation phase Use this when deciding whether the agent should invoke advisor reasoning at all. Possible labels: - `continue` - `escalate_to_advisor` - `need_more_context` - `low_confidence` ### 2) Review phase Use this after a turn or at agent end to judge whether the work is on track. Possible labels: - `on_track` - `course_correct` - `not_done` - `abstain` ### 3) Guardrails phase Use this for fast command-risk / safety decisions. Possible labels: - `allow` - `warn` - `block` - `ask` ## How it should go in the repo The best fit is a **shared policy package** that accepts: - `phase` - `task text` - optional recent context and returns: - `decision` - `confidence` - `reason` - optional `needs_escalation` Then: - `advisor` calls it at preflight/review points - `guardrails` calls it for risky shell decisions - `repo-arch` could optionally use it later for workflow routing ## Why this is better than one flat model Because the decision logic differs by phase: - preflight is about **whether to spend advisor budget** - review is about **whether work is complete/needs correction** - guardrails is about **allowing or stopping commands** So the shared model should be trained as a **phase-conditioned policy model** for the bundle, not as a single universal classifier. ## Final recommendation Package it separately from the bundle root, and make it the shared decision layer for: - `advisor` - `guardrails` - optionally `repo-arch` That keeps the bundle clean and gives you one pretrained decision layer prepared for distribution across the extensions that actually need it. community research 1: Research complete. Deliverable: [advisor-mode-research.md](/FIA/issues/FIA-268#document-research) ## Summary ### How Claude Code does it - **`/advisor` slash command:** Configure a secondary model (Sonnet/Opus). The primary model **self-invokes** when it detects it’s stuck—no manual user trigger. - **Advisor API tool:** Executor models call an `advisor` tool with empty input. Anthropic forwards the **full transcript** server-side to an Opus advisor. Result returns as `advisor_tool_result` and the executor continues. - **Best pairing:** Sonnet main + Opus advisor. Achieves near-Opus quality at near-Sonnet cost. - **Subagents vs Advisor:** `/advisor` is for inline second opinions (same framing); subagents are for fresh perspectives (clean context windows). ### How Codex does it - **Mid-session model switching** via `/model`, `/fast`, reasoning effort, and service tier (`flex` for batch, `fast` for interactive). History carries forward. - **Subagent model routing:** Each `.codex/agents/*.toml` subagent declares its own model, creating a natural cost hierarchy. - **Reasoning sandwich:** High effort for planning, standard for execution, high for verification. ### How Copilot does it - **Auto mode:** Routes each turn to the best model based on real-time health/performance signals. - **Cascade routing:** Start cheap (Sonnet), run tests/lint/types, escalate to Opus only after 2 failures. The test suite is the routing signal. - Cost multipliers compound across agent actions: Opus costs 3× per action, so a 50-action task is 150× premium requests vs Sonnet. ### General patterns - **Anthropic Advisor Strategy:** Opus as selective supervisor, Haiku/Sonnet as executor. Reduces costs ~12% while improving hard-task quality. - **NadirClaw:** Open-source proxy with ~10 ms embedding classifier, three-tier routing, and modifier heuristics (agentic/reasoning/vision/context-window detection). ## Recommendations for pi Given pi already supports mid-session model switching, custom providers (Ollama), `CustomMessageEntry` for extension-injected context messages, and prompt templates as slash commands, the advisor mode can be implemented as follows: 1. **Configuration:** Add `advisor.enabled`, `advisor.provider`, `advisor.model`, `advisor.maxCallsPerSession` to `settings.json`. 2. **Commands:** `/advisor` to toggle/configure; `/advisor ask` to force a call. 3. **Hybrid triggers:** - Prompt-based self-assessment (inject a system-prompt paragraph telling the local model to output `\ADVISOR` before substantive work when uncertain). - Rule-based pre-filter (keywords like “refactor architecture”, “security review”). - Cascade backpressure (auto-escalate after N failed test/lint attempts). - Low-confidence structured output from the local model. 4. **Context forwarding:** Build a **curated briefing** (user request + exploration summary + stuck point + relevant file micro-summaries) rather than dumping the full transcript, respecting local-model context limits. 5. **Integration modes:** - **Inline advisor:** Advisor response injected as `CustomMessageEntry` with `customType: "advisor"`; local model continues. - **Subagent advisor:** Temporary fork session with clean context for fresh perspective. 6. **Cost guards:** Client-side call cap, visible TUI counter, independent `thinkingLevel` for advisor. 7. **PoC path:** Build as a pi **extension** first—registers `/advisor`, uses existing `buildSessionContext()` + `@mariozechner/pi-ai` provider abstraction, appends advisor result via `appendCustomMessageEntry()`. Keeps the experiment self-contained before any core changes. The full research document with detailed tables, API behavior, and source links is attached as `advisor-mode-research.md` in the issue workspace. community research 2: Yes — there are already multiple pi extensions that implement advisor mode, model routing, and delegation. Here are the most relevant ones: ## 1. `pi-advisor` by RimuruW — The closest match to your ask **Direct implementation of the Anthropic advisor pattern for pi.** - **GitHub:** [RimuruW/pi-advisor](https://github.com/RimuruW/pi-advisor) - **NPM:** `pi install npm:pi-advisor` **What it does:** - Adds an LLM-callable `advisor` tool to pi (the executor model can self-invoke it). - Uses a **stronger model** (default: `anthropic/claude-opus-4-6`) as advisor. - **Stage-aware:** Automatically detects if executor is in `initial` (exploring), `recovery` (stuck/failed), or `final-check` (verification needed) and tailors the advisor prompt. - **Curated context:** Sends bounded system prompt + recent tool activity + first+last N messages (not full dump) to keep token usage efficient. - **Quota guards:** `maxUsesPerRun` (default 3), client-side cap. Rendered inline with token usage counter. - **Slash commands:** `/advisor on`, `/advisor off`, `/advisor config`, `/advisor ask` - **Configuration persists** to `~/.pi/agent/advisor.json` **Architecture:** ``` Executor (local/cheap model) ──calls advisor()──► Advisor Extension │ ▼ Detect stage Build curated briefing Call advisor model via pi-ai Return verdict + action items │ ▼ Executor continues with advice ``` The extension injects executor guidance into the system prompt telling the model WHEN to call advisor: - `INITIAL`: After 2-3 exploratory reads, before committing - `RECOVERY`: When stuck or failed - `FINAL`: After implementation + verification, before declaring done - Skip for simple tasks completable in <5 tool calls --- ## 2. `pi-model-router` by yeliu84 — Per-Turn Automatic Routing **Intelligent per-turn model router that auto-selects LLM tier.** - **GitHub:** [yeliu84/pi-model-router](https://github.com/yeliu84/pi-model-router) - **NPM:** `pi install npm:@yeliu84/pi-model-router` **What it does:** - Registers a `router` provider with stable profiles (`router/auto`, `router/cheap`, `router/deep`). - **Per-turn routing:** Automatically chooses `high` / `medium` / `low` tier for every user prompt based on task intent. - Uses **keyword heuristics** + optional LLM intent classifier + conversation phase memory. - **Cost budgeting:** Session spend limit; downgrades tier once exceeded. - **Fallback chains:** Automatic retry with alternative models if primary fails. - **Context trigger:** Auto-upgrades to high tier when token usage exceeds threshold. - **Phase bias:** Sticky tier during multi-turn planning/implementation to avoid ping-pong. --- ## 3. `pi-subagents` by nicobailon — Async Subagent Delegation **Full multi-agent orchestration with specialized child agents.** - **GitHub:** [nicobailon/pi-subagents](https://github.com/nicobailon/pi-subagents) - **NPM:** `pi install npm:pi-subagents` **Built-in agents:** | Agent | Purpose | |-------|---------| | `oracle` | Second opinion before acting — challenges assumptions | | `reviewer` | Code review against task/plan/tests | | `worker` | Implementation with approved plan | | `planner` | Implementation planning (read-only) | | `scout` | Fast codebase recon | | `researcher` | Web/docs research | Each agent can run on a **different model** (e.g., `oracle` on Opus, `worker` on Sonnet). Supports foreground/background execution, parallel chains, and session sharing. --- ## 4. `pi-model-switch` by nicobailon — Agent-Self Model Switching - **GitHub:** [nicobailon/pi-model-switch](https://github.com/nicobailon/pi-model-switch) - Lets the pi agent itself decide to switch models mid-session. Say "use Claude for this" or "switch to a cheaper model" and the agent handles it. --- ## 5. `omniroute-pi-extension` by oscar-haha — OmniRoute Gateway Integration - **GitHub:** [oscar-haha/omniroute-pi-extension](https://github.com/oscar-haha/omniroute-pi-extension) - Integrates [OmniRoute](https://omniroute.io/) — routes across 44+ LLM providers with automatic fallback, load balancing, and cost optimization. - Status bar shows which model actually served each response (e.g., `CheapFix → gemini-2.5-flash`). --- ## Recommendation For your exact use case (unlimited local LLM flow + delegate to SOTA advisor when really needed), **`pi-advisor`** is the closest fit. It: - Uses the **executor's own judgment** to trigger advisor (like Claude Code's `/advisor`) - Forwards a **curated briefing** (not full transcript dump) - Has **quota guards** (`maxUsesPerRun`) - Supports **any provider/model** via pi's model registry - Renders cleanly in pi's TUI with token counters If you also want **automatic per-turn routing** without explicit advisor calls, combine with **`pi-model-router`** for a two-layer system: 1. `pi-model-router` decides cheap vs medium vs premium per turn 2. `pi-advisor` handles the high-stakes moments within a cheap-turn session
